input: Create a video storyboard for a 60-second SaaS product demo of Fieldwork, a project management tool for construction teams. Key features: daily job-site check-ins via mobile, auto-generated progress reports, photo documentation. Audience: construction project managers who distrust software. Tone: practical, no hype.
rubric: exactly 6 scenes
scene 1 contains a hook that establishes the pain point within first 5s
final scene contains a clear CTA with URL or next step
scene durations sum to 60s ±6s
each scene includes a caption or on-screen text direction
each scene includes a visual direction (shot type, what is shown on screen)

input: Create a 30-second video storyboard for a DTC product launch of Sela, a refillable glass skincare bottle system. Hero product: $49 starter kit. Target: eco-conscious women 25–38 who already buy clean beauty. Platform: Instagram Reels. No voiceover — visuals + text overlays only. Mood: minimal, aspirational.
rubric: exactly 6 scenes
scene 1 opens with a hook visual (problem or desire) in first 3s
final scene has a CTA overlay (e.g., 'Shop now — link in bio')
scene durations sum to 30s ±3s
each scene specifies text overlay content or 'no text'
each scene describes the visual composition (close-up, wide, motion direction)

input: Storyboard a 90-second founder story video for Marcus Osei, who left a VP role at Salesforce to start Tally, a payroll app for solopreneurs. Core narrative: he was paying $80/mo for payroll software he used 12 minutes a month. Tally costs $9. Platform: LinkedIn. Talking-head format with B-roll cutaways.
rubric: exactly 6 scenes
scene 1 hook establishes the contrast or tension (before/after setup) within first 10s
final scene ends with a CTA (follow, visit site, or try free)
scene durations sum to 90s ±9s
each scene notes whether it is talking-head or B-roll
each scene includes caption/subtitle direction or on-screen text

input: Create a 45-second storyboard for a recipe short: brown butter tahini cookies. Platform: TikTok / YouTube Shorts. Style: satisfying process video, no face on camera, ASMR-adjacent. Include the ingredient reveal, mixing, baking, and the pull-apart money shot. End with a follow CTA.
rubric: exactly 6 scenes
scene 1 hook shows the finished product or a sensory moment within first 3s
final scene includes a follow or save CTA
scene durations sum to 45s ±4s
each scene includes audio direction (music genre/tempo, or ASMR sound description)
each scene describes camera angle and subject focus

input: Storyboard a 2-minute explainer video for Canopy, a B2B SaaS tool that auto-detects shadow IT usage across a company's Slack and email. Target viewer: IT security managers. Animation style: flat 2D motion graphics, no live action. Script must cover the problem, solution mechanism, integration steps, and social proof.
rubric: exactly 6 scenes
scene 1 hook names the problem (shadow IT risk) within first 10s
final scene contains CTA (book a demo / start free trial)
scene durations sum to 120s ±12s
each scene specifies animation type or motion graphic description
each scene includes narrator script line or on-screen headline

input: Create a 75-second storyboard for a YouTube ad (skippable, pre-roll) for Birchwood Supply Co., a DTC brand selling premium woodworking hand tools. Product focus: their $130 card scraper set. Audience: serious hobbyist woodworkers who buy Lie-Nielsen and Veritas. Must earn attention in the first 5 seconds before the skip button appears. No discount messaging.
rubric: exactly 6 scenes
scene 1 must be designed to prevent skip — hook within first 5s via visual or statement
final scene has CTA (visit site or learn more)
scene durations sum to 75s ±7s
each scene describes on-screen visuals with enough detail to brief a videographer
each scene notes whether voiceover, music, or natural sound is the audio layer

input: Storyboard a 60-second video for a nonprofit awareness campaign: Clean Slate Project, which provides free record-expungement legal help to formerly incarcerated people. Platform: Facebook. Audience: community advocates and donors 35–60. Emotional arc: despair → hope → action. Real testimonial format (actor re-enactment, not actual client).
rubric: exactly 6 scenes
scene 1 hook establishes stakes or emotional tension within first 8s
final scene contains a donation or volunteer CTA
scene durations sum to 60s ±6s
each scene notes the emotional beat it is meant to land
each scene describes on-screen subjects and setting

input: Create a 50-second storyboard for an onboarding welcome video for new users of Heron, a personal finance app. The video plays the first time the user opens the app after sign-up. Goal: show the 3 core actions (link bank, set a goal, read your first insight) and reduce early churn. No voiceover — screen recordings + animated callouts only.
rubric: exactly 6 scenes
scene 1 hook congratulates the user and previews the value they are about to get
final scene ends with an in-app CTA button direction (label + placement)
scene durations sum to 50s ±5s
each scene is a screen recording or animated UI direction, not live action
each scene specifies the animated callout text or tooltip copy
